platformMix.note: Viewer share uses GA4 active users grouped by stream and device data where available. People who use more than one platform may appear in more than one bucket. Apple TV is not separately broken out yet; Amazon/Fire TV is inferred from Amazon AFT smart-TV device models until the developer confirms a cleaner Fire TV label.

groups.detail: Browser and web playback from the electricnow-web GA4 stream.
iPhone and iPad app activity; Apple TV may also be included here until it is tagged separately.
Android phone/tablet activity from the ElectricNOW Android GA4 stream.
Roku activity from the electricnow-roku GA4 stream.
Likely Fire TV activity inferred from Amazon AFT smart-TV model codes.
Android TV / Google TV stream activity, excluding likely Fire TV devices where GA4 exposes Amazon AFT models.
Not separately broken out yet. It is likely inside the Apple app stream until the developer sends a distinct Apple TV platform or stream.

plainEnglishInsights: Overall, ElectricNOW’s latest complete week (May 25-May 31, 2026) shows a measured platform audience of 7,757 GA4 active users excluding landing-page acquisition, up +1.1% from the prior week. Viewing-event users and engaged hours also moved up slightly, so the late-night GA4 refresh still points to modest growth rather than a drop, but engagement rate remains a little softer than the prior week.

Late GA4 fill-in was small but real: property-wide sessions are now 14,484, engaged sessions 11,527, page/screen views 29,296, and events 1,003,933. Compared with the 12:47 PM pull, that added 6 sessions, 3 engaged sessions, 7 views, and 87 events.

Landing-page traffic remains acquisition-only. The platform-audience number above excludes the landing page; property-wide GA4 active users including acquisition context were 7,824 this week versus 7,742 the prior week.

Viewing behavior improved slightly: viewing-event users were 2,734, up +2.2% week over week; on-demand start users were 2,598, and live-channel viewers were 156. Total engaged hours were 5,407, up +2.9%.

Paid acquisition - simple read: Meta remains promising but got a little less efficient in the May 31-Jun 6 report. Nathan/Forge One reported $3,128.73 of Meta spend, 248,978 paid-campaign impressions, 7,970 link clicks, and 2,997 downloads at about $1.04 blended cost per download. Android remains cheaper ($0.89/download) than iOS ($1.34/download), but both platform CPIs worsened versus the prior report, so the next priority is proving these installs become actual viewers before scaling spend.

Google Ads remains marked stale for this refresh because the connector was rate-limited earlier today; the dashboard preserves the last successful pull through 2026-05-26 rather than zeroing it out. Treat Google Ads as directional until the next successful connector pull.

Apple App Store Connect manual download context was updated from the user screenshots: Free iOS/watchOS/tvOS downloads were 1,343 in the U.S. for May 31-Jun 6, 2026, with a daily average of 192. The separate five-week Apple snapshot shows 6,183 total downloads for Apr 27-May 31, up 353%. These are downloads only; they are not Apple sales and are not counted as GA4 active users.

Visible platform sales remain preserved and audited at 43 purchases / $806.57 year to date across Stripe, Roku, and manual Apple sales snapshots. This is still not total ElectricNOW sales because Google Play sales are not connected and Apple sales are manual.

Bottom line: the app/platform story is still cautiously positive, but paid acquisition is no longer improving cleanly. ElectricNOW is holding roughly steady on measured usage, Roku app engagement remains meaningful at 261 average daily viewers and 2,285 hours streamed for May 30-Jun 5, and Apple/Meta downloads show real acquisition volume; the key question remains how many of those paid downloads turn into repeat viewing.
instrumentationGaps: No separate GA4 event found for go_ad_free_click yet.
No separate GA4 event found for video_purchase yet.
No separate GA4 event found for collection_purchase yet.

Stripe is now one visible sales source in the dashboard; total sales still require Apple App Store and Google Play reporting, and GA4 purchase events still need to be split into go-ad-free, individual video, and collection purchase actions.
Live TV should be reconciled with Amagi and ad-server reporting.

The increase over the prior visible 13/$189.87 is historical 2026 charges that became visible after reconnection, not new same-day sales. Apple App Store and Google Play purchases are handled separately.

live.note: Live usage is based on GA4 screen name 'Live' plus playback events that also carried the Live screen name.

onDemand.note: On Demand usage is based on GA4 screen name 'On Demand'. Title-level viewing is shown below from GA4 video_title play events.

titleViewership.note: Top programs roll up fragmented GA4 play-event labels into parent shows/collections where possible, then use a GA4 filter to estimate deduped active users for each program. Play events remain the ranking metric because ads monetize playback; active users are total GA4 users who triggered matching play events for that program during the period. On-demand shows and collections only — live/FAST/linear channels (DARK MATTER TV, Kung Fu Movies, spot on news, ToonOvation, ElectricNOW en Espanol, Stoner TV Network, etc.) are excluded here and reported separately under Live channel usage. Top 50 may list fewer than 50 entries until the next GA4 refresh overfetches with the stricter row-level predicate. Treat this as GA-tracked viewership, not a fully cleaned catalog report yet, until the app sends content_type, series_title, season, episode, and video_id consistently.
titleViewership.topVideoTitlesNote: Top videos shows individual on-demand video plays only. Live/FAST/linear channel rows (e.g. DARK MATTER TV, Kung Fu Movies, spot on news, ToonOvation, ElectricNOW en Espanol, Stoner TV Network) are excluded here and reported separately in 'Live channel usage'. Top 50 may list fewer than 50 entries until the next GA4 refresh overfetches with the stricter row-level live-channel predicate.

metaCampaignContext.note: Meta campaign objective shifted from landing-page traffic, to app-store button-click leads, to direct app-install campaigns starting Apr 28. The app-install phase bypasses the website landing page, so lower landing-page traffic is expected and should not be judged as a standalone negative.
metaCampaignContext.appInstallBreakout: Through May 3, Meta reported 516 iOS installs at about $0.65 each and 949 Google Play installs at about $0.41 each.
metaCampaignContext.interpretation: Judge the newest phase by installs, app opens, viewing, Live usage, purchases, and ad value rather than by landing-page traffic.

Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

appSectionUsage.metric: GA4 active users and engaged time

rows.definition: Home/content browsing activity inferred from GA4 screen and path labels such as Home, On Demand, PDP, Player, Category, and Search.
Spanish-language section activity inferred from GA4 labels containing En Espanol, Español, Espanol, Spanish, or matching paths.
Live TV activity inferred from GA4 Live screen/path labels.
Saved or personal-list activity inferred from My Stuff, My List, Mylist, Watchlist, and Favorites labels.
Settings/account activity inferred from Settings, Account, Profile, and Preferences labels.

appSectionUsage.note: This section uses the current GA4 screen/path labels to infer the five app navigation areas in the order Home, En Espanol, Live TV, My Stuff, and Settings. A user can appear in more than one section. For cleaner reporting, the app should send a stable app_section value with exactly these five section names.

rokuAppEngagement.sourceDetail: Roku app engagement is parsed from the latest Roku App Engagement PDF emailed from bdp_noreply@data.roku.com. It measures Roku app/channel-store usage and is shown separately from GA4 app/web metrics.
rokuAppEngagement.rawReportPeriod: 8 days ago for 7 days Time Grain is day
rokuAppEngagement.note: Roku TRC Live Dashboard reports are intentionally excluded from app/platform usage to avoid mixing Roku Channel live-stream consumption with ElectricNOW app engagement.

visitSummary.source: GA4 sessions on tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths.
visitSummary.note: Visits are GA4 sessions, not unique people. Weekly visits use the latest 7 complete days and compare against the previous 7 complete days. Landing-page traffic remains acquisition-only and is not included here.

manualAppleSalesUpdate.note: Preserved May 25 Apple sale (Purchase HD 15, $14.99 gross). 4 manual entries: May 13, May 18 (2), May 25.

googleAdsSummary.plainEnglish: Google Ads is now useful for judging paid Android app-campaign efficiency directly: it shows spend, clicks, installs/first-opens, conversion rate, and cost per conversion. This should sit beside GA4 viewing quality so we can see not only whether ads are buying installs, but whether those installs become viewers.
googleAdsSummary.dataFreshnessNote: STALE THIS RUN: Google Ads connector rate-limited on 2026-06-01; preserving last successful campaign totals through May 26, 2026. Device and conversion-action breakouts are from the prior detailed segment pull.
googleAdsSummary.statusDetail: Google Ads connector was rate-limited on the 2026-06-01 refresh (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, retry delay ~228s). Showing the last successful connector pull through May 26, 2026. Figures are not zeroed; they are the most recent good data and should be treated as stale until the next successful pull.

googleAds.note: Paid acquisition looks efficient on cost, but the growth signal is mixed: Google Ads spent $241.08 from May 12-May 26, 2026, generated 46,962 paid-campaign impressions, 1,458 clicks, and 443 Android first-open conversions at about $0.54 per first open. That cost per first open is strong for app acquisition, but platform audience was down 8.0% and viewing was down 2.7% in the latest GA4 week, so the ads appear good at buying installs/opens but we still need to watch whether those paid users convert into sustained viewers and time spent. Meta remains the bigger spend driver in the latest Nathan report (May 24-May 30) at $3,146.72 for 3,259 downloads (about $0.97 blended cost per download), with Android still cheaper than iOS; iOS cost per download improved to $1.23 while Android rose to $0.80, and impressions/link clicks softened (down 4.8% and 13.1%). The next question for Patrick/Nathan is whether the lower-cost Android volume produces real viewing quality.
googleAds.staleNote: Google Ads connector rate-limited on 2026-06-01; last successful pull May 12-May 26, 2026. Values preserved (not zeroed) and marked stale.

metaAds.source: Nathan / Forge One Meta email report
metaAds.note: Meta paid acquisition for May 31-Jun 6 remains acceptable but less efficient than the prior week. Spend was essentially flat at $3,128.73 and the campaigns produced 2,997 reported downloads at about $1.04 blended cost per download. Android is still the stronger efficiency story: 1,961 Android downloads at $0.89 each, while iOS delivered 1,036 downloads at $1.34 each. The concern is that both iOS volume and cost moved the wrong way: iOS downloads fell 17.9% and cost per iOS download rose 7.4%; Android volume was basically flat but cost per Android download rose 11.3%. The good news is CTR remains healthy at about 3.20%, so the creative/audience is still generating interest. Verdict: promising but needs proof of downstream viewing. Next tests should keep Android scaled, tighten or refresh iOS creative, and compare these paid downloads against GA4 viewing and Roku/Apple app engagement before increasing budget materially.

It covers Facebook and Instagram app-install campaigns for ElectricNOW and is separate from Google Ads connector data. These are paid-campaign impressions and clicks, not in-app ad impressions served inside ElectricNOW.
metaAdsSummary.paidAcquisitionInterpretation: Meta paid acquisition for May 31-Jun 6 remains acceptable but less efficient than the prior week. Spend was essentially flat at $3,128.73 and the campaigns produced 2,997 reported downloads at about $1.04 blended cost per download. Android is still the stronger efficiency story: 1,961 Android downloads at $0.89 each, while iOS delivered 1,036 downloads at $1.34 each. The concern is that both iOS volume and cost moved the wrong way: iOS downloads fell 17.9% and cost per iOS download rose 7.4%; Android volume was basically flat but cost per Android download rose 11.3%. The good news is CTR remains healthy at about 3.20%, so the creative/audience is still generating interest. Verdict: promising but needs proof of downstream viewing. Next tests should keep Android scaled, tighten or refresh iOS creative, and compare these paid downloads against GA4 viewing and Roku/Apple app engagement before increasing budget materially.

ga4PurchaseSignals.sourceDetail: These are GA4 in_app_purchase events from property 497892271. They are directional purchase signals only — GA4 does not return Google Play / Android billing transaction IDs, so these rows must be reconciled against Google Play Console before being treated as financial revenue. They are intentionally kept out of the Stripe / Roku / Apple visible-sales rollup above.
ga4PurchaseSignals.reconciliationLabel: GA4 purchase signal, not reconciled revenue
ga4PurchaseSignals.reconciliationDetail: Likely Google Play / Android billing. Reconcile against Google Play Console before counting toward final sales. Do not double count if the same purchase later appears in Stripe, Roku, Apple, or official Google Play reporting.

latestDay.label: GA4 in-app purchase signals on May 21, 2026

comparisonProperty.result: No in_app_purchase rows returned for 2026-05-21 from the older GA4 property; signal is only present in property 497892271.

ga4PurchaseSignals.note: GA4 detected $29.98 of Android in-app purchase activity on May 21, 2026 across 2 in_app_purchase events (1 Android mobile in Philadelphia at appVersion 16.019; 1 Android smart TV in Vancouver, WA at appVersion 16.026). This is most likely Google Play / Android billing. It is shown separately from Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot and is NOT included in visible platform sales until it is reconciled with Google Play Console.

manualAppleDownloads.sourceDetail: User-provided App Store Connect screenshots for Free iOS/watchOS/tvOS downloads. This is app download activity, not paid sales and not GA4 active users.
manualAppleDownloads.note: Because this is a manual Apple Connect screenshot, it should be preserved until replaced by a newer screenshot or an Apple App Store Connect API integration. Daily U.S. values total 1,343 for May 31-Jun 6; the separate 5-week screen shows 6,183 total free iOS/watchOS/tvOS downloads for Apr 27-May 31.

manualDataPatch.note: Manual/email data patch applied without changing GA4, Stripe, Roku sales, or Apple sales totals.
